title: Trigonometric identities
description: Verify the Pythagorean identity; use it to find unknown trig ratios given one ratio and the quadrant; apply the identities $1 + \tan^2\theta = \sec^2\theta$ and $1 + \cot^2\theta = \csc^2\theta$; use complementary angle identities; prove two-step identities by expressing everything in terms of $\sin\theta$ and $\cos\theta$.
